messages.author: gjelsoe
messages.text: You could do something like : ⁨ #define THERMISTOR_PIN 14 #define SERIES_RESISTOR 10000 // 10K ohm resistor #define THERMISTOR_NOMINAL 10000 // NTC resistance at 25°C #define TEMPERATURE_NOMINAL 25 // Temperature for nominal resistance #define B_COEFFICIENT 3380 // Beta coefficient for NCP18XH103F03RB #define ADC_MAX 4095 // 12-bit ADC #define VCC 3.3 // Supply voltage ⁩ And setting analog resolution : ⁨ analogReadResolution(12); // Set ADC to 12-bit resolution ⁩ Reading values and converting : ⁨ // Read ADC value (average of multiple readings for stability) float adcValue = 0; for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) { adcValue += analogRead(THERMISTOR_PIN); delay(10); } adcValue /= 10.0; // Convert ADC reading to voltage float voltage = (adcValue / ADC_MAX) * VCC; // Calculate thermistor resistance using voltage divider formula // For pull-up configuration: NTC is connected to VCC, resistor to GND // Vout = Vin * (R_series / (R_series + R_NTC)) // R_NTC = R_series * ((Vin / Vout) - 1) float resistance = SERIES_RESISTOR * ((VCC / voltage) - 1); // Calculate temperature using Steinhart-Hart simplified Beta equation // 1/T = 1/T0 + (1/B) * ln(R/R0) float steinhart; steinhart = resistance / THERMISTOR_NOMINAL; // (R/R0) steinhart = log(steinhart); // ln(R/R0) steinhart /= B_COEFFICIENT; // 1/B * ln(R/R0) steinhart += 1.0 / (TEMPERATURE_NOMINAL + 273.15); // + (1/T0) steinhart = 1.0 / steinhart; // Invert float temperatureC = steinhart - 273.15; // Convert to Celsius float temperatureF = temperatureC * 9.0 / 5.0 + 32.0; // Convert to Fahrenheit ⁩
messages.content_html: You could do something like : ⁨#define THERMISTOR_PIN 14 #define SERIES_RESISTOR 10000 // 10K ohm resistor #define THERMISTOR_NOMINAL 10000 // NTC resistance at 25°C #define TEMPERATURE_NOMINAL 25 // Temperature for nominal resistance #define B_COEFFICIENT 3380 // Beta coefficient for NCP18XH103F03RB #define ADC_MAX 4095 // 12-bit ADC #define VCC 3.3 // Supply voltage⁩ And setting analog resolution : ⁨analogReadResolution(12); // Set ADC to 12-bit resolution⁩ Reading values and converting : ⁨ // Read ADC value (average of multiple readings for stability) float adcValue = 0; for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) { adcValue += analogRead(THERMISTOR_PIN); delay(10); } adcValue /= 10.0; // Convert ADC reading to voltage float voltage = (adcValue / ADC_MAX) * VCC; // Calculate thermistor resistance using voltage divider formula // For pull-up configuration: NTC is connected to VCC, resistor to GND // Vout = Vin * (R_series / (R_series + R_NTC)) // R_NTC = R_series * ((Vin / Vout) - 1) float resistance = SERIES_RESISTOR * ((VCC / voltage) - 1); // Calculate temperature using Steinhart-Hart simplified Beta equation // 1/T = 1/T0 + (1/B) * ln(R/R0) float steinhart; steinhart = resistance / THERMISTOR_NOMINAL; // (R/R0) steinhart = log(steinhart); // ln(R/R0) steinhart /= B_COEFFICIENT; // 1/B * ln(R/R0) steinhart += 1.0 / (TEMPERATURE_NOMINAL + 273.15); // + (1/T0) steinhart = 1.0 / steinhart; // Invert float temperatureC = steinhart - 273.15; // Convert to Celsius float temperatureF = temperatureC * 9.0 / 5.0 + 32.0; // Convert to Fahrenheit⁩

messages.author: griffc.
messages.text: I've slopped up some changes and have got my board working again: Fixed progressive deafness — SX1262 register 0x8B5 patch now applied on init and after every AGC reset - AGC reset enabled by default — recalibrates the RX frontend every 60 seconds Proper LNA sequencing — RXEN pin now correctly managed around TX to prevent the LNA being left in a bad state Clean power cycle on boot — 50ms hard reset of the SX1262 module ensures a fresh start after soft reboot Will monitor. Re AGC reset - i have other TXs close by which might have turned the AGC down to such an extent it became totally deaf (edited)
messages.content_html:
I've slopped up some changes and have got my board working again:
  • Fixed progressive deafness — SX1262 register 0x8B5 patch now applied on init and after every AGC reset - AGC reset enabled by default — recalibrates the RX frontend every 60 seconds
  • Proper LNA sequencing — RXEN pin now correctly managed around TX to prevent the LNA being left in a bad state
  • Clean power cycle on boot — 50ms hard reset of the SX1262 module ensures a fresh start after soft reboot
Will monitor. Re AGC reset - i have other TXs close by which might have turned the AGC down to such an extent it became totally deaf (edited)
messages.reply_html:

anyone had issues with their boards going extremely deaf after running as a repeater for a while? the TX and PA side still work but the LNA RX path is cooked. im wondering if the sequencing could be off and the LNA is exposed to the PA - cooking it over time. Theres a warning in the lilygo repo about the possibility of the PA cooking itself but not the LNA
